items.q: How do you say 'I am going to Spain' in French?
items.why: Students confuse aller (to go - movement away from speaker) and venir (to come - movement towards speaker). 'Je vais' = I'm going; 'je viens' = I'm coming. Also: en + feminine country, au + masculine, aux + plural.

items.q: What is the 'vous' form of 'être' in the present tense?
items.why: Students apply -ez ending from regular verbs. Être is irregular: je suis, tu es, il est, nous sommes, vous ÊTES, ils sont. Memorisation is essential - être appears constantly.
A classic faux ami. Actuellement looks like 'actually' but means CURRENTLY. To say 'actually' use en fait or en réalité. These false friends regularly cost marks.

items.q: Which sentence correctly says 'I get on well with my brother'?
items.correct: Je m'entends bien avec mon frère.
items.misconception: Je m'entend bien avec mon frère.
items.random_wrong: Je entends bien avec mon frère.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Je m'entends bien avec mon frère.. “Je m'entend bien avec mon frère.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which form of 'you' should you use when addressing your French teacher?
items.why: It's vous. “tu” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many forms does the French adjective 'beau' have (masc sing, masc plur, fem sing, fem plur, masc before vowel 'bel')?
items.why: The real figure is 5 forms. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
